title: Tabernaemontana capuronii
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana capuronii belongs to the family Apocynaceae and the order Gentianales. It is a species of flowering plant within the genus Tabernaemontana. The genus Tabernaemontana is further classified under the subfamily Rauvolfioideae, which includes many species of tropical plants. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes approximately 4,900 species. This plant's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Tabernaemontana capuronii, also known as the Madagascar yellow wood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana capuronii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and tinctures.
